name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Rose and Eliza
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
I was recently given documents showing that both Amy and I are descended
from people who owned slaves. Along with other possessions listed in their
property log were two human beings, Rose and Eliza.

A paternal great-great-great grandfather of mine, Andrew Cowan Jasper,
owned these two women in the 1850s. There are also records showing that a
maternal great-great-great grandfather, Frederick Williams, most likely
owned slaves in the 1860s (“most likely,” because we are not certain that
the Frederick Williams who is my ancestor and the Frederick Williams who
owned slaves are the same person, but there’s enough circumstantial data
to lead me to conclude that it’s likely).

Records also showed that Amy had an ancestor who owned slaves and another
who was a member of the Confederate Army.

Something that we’ve been thinking about and talking about in town hall
meetings and out on the campaign — the legacy of slavery in the United
States — now has a much more personal connection.

Ownership of other human beings conferred advantages not just to Andrew
Jasper and Frederick Williams, but to Jasper’s and Williams’ descendants
as well. They were able to build wealth on the backs and off the sweat of
others, wealth that they would then be able to pass down to their children
and their children’s children. In some way, and in some form, that
advantage would pass through to me and my children.

That those enslaved Americans owned by my ancestors were denied their
freedom, denied the ability to amass wealth, denied full civil rights in
America after slavery also had long term repercussions for them and their
descendants.

The way that fortune was passed through the generations from Andrew to me,
misfortune was passed through the generations from Rose and Eliza to their
descendants who are alive today. Rose and Eliza were denied their freedom
and the benefits that their labor produced; they and their children were
then denied their civil rights after the end of Reconstruction; and their
descendants endured open terrorism, economic exclusion and racism in the
form of Jim Crow, lynchings, convict leasing, voter suppression, red
lining, predatory lending, and mass incarceration. Everything their
descendants have accomplished in their lives is despite having all of
these odds stacked against them.

In the aggregate, slavery, its legacy and the ensuing forms of
institutionalized racism have produced an America with stark differences
in opportunities and outcomes, depending on race.

For example, there is 10 times the wealth in white American than there is
in black America. Black men are six times more likely to be incarcerated
than white men. The disparity in infant mortality between black families
and white families is greater today than it was in 1850. Whether it’s the
economy, healthcare, education, criminal justice or even in the inherent
biases revealed by technology, there really are two Americas.

I benefit from a system that my ancestors built to favor themselves at the
expense of others. That only increases the urgency I feel to help change
this country so that it works for those who have been locked-out of — or
locked-up in — this system.

As a person, as a candidate for the office of the Presidency, I will do
everything I can to deliver on this responsibility.

In addition to making significant changes to education policy (immediately
address $23 billion in underfunding for minority-majority public schools),
economic policy (ensuring equal pay, deploying capital to minority- and
women-owned businesses, $25 billion in government procurement to these
same businesses), healthcare (universal healthcare and home health visits
to women of color to reverse trend in maternal and infant mortality) and
criminal justice (police accountability, ending the drug war, and
expunging arrest records for nonviolent drug crimes), I will continue to
support reparations, beginning with an important national conversation on
slavery and racial injustice.

We all need to know our own story as it relates to the national story,
much as I am learning mine. It is only then, I believe, that we can take
the necessary steps to repair the damage done and stop visiting this
injustice on the generations that follow ours.

- Beto

name: Mike Siegel
subject: I stand with Ilhan
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE SIEGEL FOR CONGRESS](https://click
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8279073/79822317/683764388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTUwZmUwMi0wNWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ueG39PAnY5RLKB0OGdBFIsKvhwsfZYgBZIfdGzcwPTI=)
---
Peter, I stand with Ilhan. If you’ve been on this list for a while, you know I don’t spend much energy talking about the President. You hear enough about Trump elsewhere, I’m sure, and I prefer to focus on the concrete work we are doing here in the Texas 10th to improve the lives of the people. But what the President said yesterday deserves comment. In essence, Trump told four members of Congress—all women, from diverse racial backgrounds—to “go back to where you came from.” A racist message, based on the assumption that only white people are “from here.” Nonsensical, to an extent, given that three of the Representatives are American-born. But also targeted: in particular at Rep. Ilhan Omar, a naturalized U.S. citizen who arrived here as a refugee from Somalia. Trump thinks that attacking Omar, who is Muslim, Black, and progressive, is good politics. We need to show the world he is dead wrong. I was lucky enough to be born in this country. But I come from a family that includes Jewish ancestors, who came here as refugees; much of our family was not so lucky, unfortunately, and perished in the Holocaust. I also come from a family that includes Protestant ancestors, who came here as refugees, fleeing religious persecution. And so many of us have similar histories: how we came here, recently or generations ago, for sanctuary and opportunity. And that, truly, is what makes America great. Despite the structural flaws in this country, the history of injustice and inequality, there is also a recurring theme: of families from around the world, from hundreds of countries and cultures, who arrived here to build a better life, not just for themselves, but for all of us. And the attack on Rep. Omar—at the same time we are locking up asylum seekers in concentration camps, and announcing nationwide “raids” to terrorize immigrant communities—is an attack on the promise of America itself. Trump knows he cannot win if the diverse, open-hearted people of this country come together. His regime only prospers through division, fear and hatred. We must fight back. We must stand up for Rep. Omar — not just because she is an important voice and leader, a loving community member and sincere and committed representative, but also because she represents the best of this country. The promise of a better life. The poetry inscribed on the Statue of Liberty, that all will be welcome within these borders. So let’s stand up for Ilhan. Let’s stand up for refugees. Let’s stand up for all of the marginalized communities in this country. Let’s unite across cultures and differences.

Together, we are strong. Unbeatable, even. And Trump knows that. Let’s make sure his days are numbered. For the people,

subject: Vice President Pence: Congress Must Pass the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement
body:
"Leaders on both sides of the aisle in Congress have long recognized the failures of the trade deal the USMCA will replace." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ae8f32c00e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Vice President Pence: Congress Must Pass the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement**

“Leaders on both sides of the aisle in Congress have long recognized the failures of the trade deal the USMCA will replace: the North American Free Trade Agreement,” Vice President Mike Pence writes in _The Washington Post_.

“That outdated trade deal has hurt middle-class Americans, stifled innovation and left entire communities shuttered as jobs and opportunity have moved south of the border. The USMCA will fix NAFTA’s problems and accomplish much more. And not only is the agreement a bipartisan win for the United States, it’s a win for all three countries.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9c13a1e4ed&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “What is going on in this country right now, especially on Capitol Hill, is nothing short of deceitful behavior which intentionally distorts the truth in order to win a political battle,” writes Thomas Homan, a top immigration official under both President Trump and former President Obama, in [**Fox News**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb9b4bdcac&e=3ee4985eea). “I saw it firsthand when I testified on Capitol Hill this past Friday, in front of the House Committee on Oversight and Reform. Aside from the six minutes I was given to read my opening statement, there was an attempt by most Democrats on that committee to silence me.”

He continued: “They didn’t want to hear from me. They didn’t want the truth. They simply wanted to continue to push their false narrative about the Border Patrol and ICE.”
---
|
---
| Today, President Donald J. Trump will require Federal agencies to buy domestic iron, steel, and other materials for projects such as airports, roads, and bridges, White House Director of Trade and Manufacturing Policy Peter Navarro writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6c3d15b32e&e=3ee4985eea). “Buy American federal procurement rules provide good manufacturing jobs at good wages, propelling more workers into middle-class prosperity . . . Perhaps most importantly, Buy American helps strengthen our manufacturing and defense industrial base.”
---
|
---
| “Congress has been sitting on the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement, or USMCA, for months. A badly needed update to NAFTA, the USMCA is in danger of being talked to death by Democratic House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, despite the free trade agreement enjoying broad bipartisan support,” tax expert Ryan Ellis writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=aa565b8ce7&e=3ee4985eea). “USMCA would create 176,000 new jobs and permanently increase the size of our economy by $68.2 billion, or 0.35%. That’s like giving $200 to every man, woman, and child in America, every year.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c50cb3623b&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a940d26b65&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=87bbf3222c)
---

name: West Virginia Democratic Party
subject: DEMO MEMO: The Hate Fueled GOP
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
**DEMO MEMO: **
The Republican Party’s racism, bigotry, and hate is at an all-time high. Trump’s tweets over the weekend attacking U.S. Reps. Ilhan Omar, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib are inexcusable, but sadly this is not an isolated incident from the president or the Republican Party.
* Not a word from Republicans in Congress. Not a word from RNC Chair Ronna McDaniel. Where is the moral compass of the Republican Party?
* Trump’s comments are not isolated; they are representative of this president’s entire agenda to devalue women’s voices and discriminate against communities of color.
* These four congresswomen are patriots who are fighting for a more perfect union for all Americans. If only we had a president who did the same.

The Republican Party leaders in West Virginia (including Chairwoman Melody Potter) have been penning op-eds, posting on social media, filing lawsuits, and doing press interviews discriminating against communities of color and the LGBTQ+ community. No one representing the Republican Party in West Virginia has condemned these actions.
**Below are some of the actions we’re referring to: **
* During Republican Party Day at the Legislature an Anti-Muslim poster of Congresswoman Ilhan Omar was displayed outside of Senate Chambers. [The poster displayed a meme with Omar under a photo of the burning Twin Towers with the words "Never Forget". The WVGOP cited freedom of speech as a response.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291828/79931363/1662085137?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=) The Republican Party approved the vendors participating.

* GOP Delegate Eric Porterfield made multiple remarks against the LGBTQ+ community during the legislative session including [comparing the community and activists to the KKK](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291829/79931364/1623746587?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=) and stated during a press interview that he would [see if his children “could swim” if they told him they were gay](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291830/79931365/-34701295?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=). No action was taken against Porterfield, and these comments were often made on the House Chamber Floor.

* GOP Senator Mike Azinger penned a [hate piece](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291831/79931366/-1448901084?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=) in the Parkersburg News and Sentinel titled “The Shame of LGBTQ Pride” [which was endorsed by the Republican Party Chairwoman](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291832/79931367/-156469253?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=). In the opinion piece, Azinger stated that “sexual deviancy is going mainstream.”

* [GOP Chairwoman Melody Potter doubled down on Azinger’s hate remarks by publicly endorsing it on her Facebook page.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291833/79931368/-156469253?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=) Fellow Republican leaders, including Senate President Mitch Carmichael and Governor Jim Justice have stayed silent and have NOT denounced the actions of their Party.

* [GOP Delegate Sharon Malcolm’s lawsuit against her former supervisor was found to be “racially motivated”](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291834/79931370/1350292258?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=) by the Bureau of Employment Programs. In the suit, Malcolm accused her supervisor, a black man, of sexual harassment and stated that he should not supervise white women. She also called it “discrimination in reverse.” She wrote in her complaint that she wanted, “Mr. Hill’s removal from Supervisor II Vance Hill Jr. supervisors position over white women. Give me his title, position, his salary plus 10%, his office”.
* [ Since then, a look into Malcolm’s social media accounts has revealed the extent of her bigotry and hate. ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291836/79931371/-746343502?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=)

* GOP Del John Mandt called the LGBTQ Community, the “alphabet group” and claimed they were [harassing his business.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291837/79931372/961457106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=) Previously Mandt refused to participate in a vigil for the 50 people killed in the two mosques in New Zealand, stating that [“Anything Muslim would be associated with Democrats.” ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8291839/79931374/-1933398722?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjhhNTEzYS00MWE3LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=csmop-NVe_VDVEyTCdI3CxeUe_c_6IGM7RmxTCaKQZg=)

Republican leaders and fellow elected Party officials have remained silent on these actions.
'

body:
|
#
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to make a donation to my presidential campaign before tomorrow’s mid-month fundraising deadline. Let me explain why: **After the debate, our campaign surged -- in the polls, with endorsements, donations, volunteers, and so much more.** It was clear that our message broke through and that millions of Americans were ready to join our campaign to prosecute the case against four more years of Donald Trump. But if we’re not careful, energy and momentum like that can be fleeting. Our polling numbers doubled in some cases almost overnight, but everything can turn on a dime if we’re not ready to build an organization that can win this primary. Tomorrow at midnight is the first mid-month fundraising deadline we’ve faced since the debate, and we’re behind where we need to be to hit our fundraising goals. If we don’t catch up, we won’t be able to capitalize on this moment. I cannot do this alone -- it’s going to take thousands of supporters like you giving what you can to help us win this election, which is why I’m asking: **Will you rush a contribution to my campaign before our mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight tomorrow? | |

There is no place for racism in America.

And yet, the President is a racist. We see it in his latest, awful tweets over the weekend. We see it in his cruelty to children and families at our border. We see it in so many of his attacks on people of color.

For decades, there was a bipartisan consensus that racism is unacceptable. But when it comes to Donald Trump, Republican silence is deafening. We need to show America that racism, and quietly condoning racism, are losing strategies. And we do that by soundly defeating Donald Trump and the Republicans who are afraid to denounce his racism.

name: John Cornyn
subject: Keeping you updated
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Texans for Senator John Cornyn, Inc
body:
I know I send you a lot of emails, most of which ask for you to chip in to show your support, or add your name to a petition.

Friend,

I know I send you a lot of emails, most of which ask for you to chip in to show your support, or add your name to a petition.

This **is not** one of those times.

Friend, this time I’m only asking you to read – because I have great news to share, and a special thank you.

I am pleased to report that this past quarter, not only did we meet our End of Quarter goal, but we did so in a way that is sure to turn heads from coast to coast, and send a clear message to those fighting to flip Texas blue – they have an uphill battle.

**We raised more this quarter then Beto did in the same quarter for his race against Senator Cruz. How’s that for momentum?**

It’s this kind of momentum that will carry us across the finish line come Election Day. It’s your support that will Keep Texas Red. Thank you, friend.

**But we are not stopping. It is likely the Democrats will spend more than $50 million against us next year and that means a lot of attacks to fend off. **

But friend, I’m counting you to remain by my side. If you’ll be with me until the very end, there’s no way we can lose.

Thank you again for all you do to support our great state, friend. It means the world.

–Senator John Cornyn

Paid for by Texans for Senator John Cornyn, Inc.

clean_body: I know I send you a lot of emails, most of which ask for you to chip in to show your support, or add your name to a petition. Friend, I know I send you a lot of emails, most of which ask for you to chip in to show your support, or add your name to a petition. This is not one of those times. Friend, this time I’m only asking you to read – because I have great news to share, and a special thank you. I am pleased to report that this past quarter, not only did we meet our End of Quarter goal, but we did so in a way that is sure to turn heads from coast to coast, and send a clear message to those fighting to flip Texas blue – they have an uphill battle. We raised more this quarter then Beto did in the same quarter for his race against Senator Cruz. How’s that for momentum? It’s this kind of momentum that will carry us across the finish line come Election Day. It’s your support that will Keep Texas Red. Thank you, friend. But we are not stopping. It is likely the Democrats will spend more than $50 million against us next year and that means a lot of attacks to fend off. But friend, I’m counting you to remain by my side. If you’ll be with me until the very end, there’s no way we can lose. Thank you again for all you do to support our great state, friend. It means the world. –Senator John Cornyn Paid for by Texans for Senator John Cornyn, Inc.

| **ICYMI: Governor Holcomb Announces
He's Running for "Four More Years"**
---
|
---
| On Saturday, Governor Holcomb made Hoosier history. Surrounded by supporters from all 92 counties, he announced that he's running for re-election as Indiana's governor.

**It's four more years of delivering results. Four more years of breaking records. And four more years of Putting People First. **

Check out some of the headlines Governor Holcomb is earning across the state:
---
|
---
| **_Indianapolis Business Journal: _
Holcomb makes re-election bid official, says ‘we’re going to do even more’**
The 51-year-old Republican announced his re-election campaign with a slogan of “Putting People First” on Saturday at the historic Hoosier Gym in Knightstown, where the movie “Hoosiers” was filmed.

He told the crowd of several hundred supporters, including a handful of Republican state lawmakers and members of Indiana’s congressional delegation, that he wanted to keep cutting down nets and keep hanging banners.
---
| [IBJ: Read the Full Story](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c715350f3a&e=1acd9bb3d6 "IBJ: Read the Full Story")
---
| |
---
| **_WFYI:_**
**Holcomb's Path To Re-election Likely An Easy One** Gov. Eric Holcomb officially launched his re-election bid Saturday and political analysts say Holcomb’s path to a second term is likely an easy one. No elected Indiana governor has lost a re-election bid since the state began allowing second terms in the 1970s. And Mike Downs Center for Indiana Politics director Andrew Downs says it’s unlikely Eric Holcomb will break that trend.
---
| [WFYI: Read the Full Story](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bce2adfd8b&e=1acd9bb3d6 "WFYI: Read the Full Story")
---
|
---
| **_Indianapolis Star:_**
**Gov. Eric Holcomb: 'Let's keep making Hoosier history for four more years'** Indiana Sen. Todd Young, who also spoke at the reelection event, said the Republican Party “continues to deliver to the American people.” Indiana’s progress has been driven by leadership, Young continued, “and Eric Holcomb is just getting started.” U.S. Rep. Greg Pence said other states “could learn a lot” from Indiana and the work Holcomb has done as governor, and Indiana Republican Party chairman Kyle Hupfer added that Holcomb “puts the interests of Hoosiers first.”
---
| [Indy Star: Read the Full Story](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4487378a07&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Indy Star: Read the Full Story")
---
|
---
| **_Fort Wayne Journal Gazette_**
**Governor Holcomb announces re-election bid** "I thought there was a tremendous amount of energy," said Allen County Republican Chairman Steve Shine. "He hit on several aspects including a thriving economy."

Shine added that it looks to be a banner year for Holcomb's re-election – "he has a great record to run on. It is stellar."
---
| [Journal Gazette: Read the Full Story](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6d90016a1c&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Journal Gazette: Read the Full Story")
---
|
---
| **_Associated Press:_
Governor Holcomb announces 2020 re-election campaign** Republican Gov. Eric Holcomb brandished his front-runner status Saturday as he kicked off his re-election campaign with a rally extolling the state's economy while brushing off any criticism of his record. Holcomb made his announcement before a few hundred people at Knightstown's Hoosier Gym, which served as the home court for the fictional underdog Hickory Huskers basketball team in the 1986 movie "Hoosiers." But he heads into the 2020 election with $6 million already in his campaign's bank account thanks to his leverage of leading a GOP-dominated state without any high-profile Democrats vying to unseat him.
---

name: Joseph Geevarghese, Our Revolution
subject: Amazon's anti-worker record
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
Today begins Amazon's self-described "2-day parade of epic deals", Prime Day. For the next two days, Amazon workers will put in back-breaking hours to deal with the increased volume moving through their warehouses, all while being threatened with docked pay if they take too long of a bathroom break.

Thanks to the work of Senator Bernie Sanders and dedicated activists like you, we have already forced Amazon to raise their employees' minimum wage to $15 an hour -- **but even though we 've won, we're not done.**

That's why Our Revolution is partnering with Warehouse Workers for Justice -- [we're organizing to demand Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos create a safe and humane working environment, slow down the pace of work to reasonable speeds, and respect their fundamental right to organize.](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/stand-with-workers?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123&city=&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=1+-+wersquore+organizing+to+demand+Amazon+CE&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190715_2_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190715_2_1")

[Sign here to tell Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos to implement Warehouse Workers for Justice's demands IMMEDIATELY ->](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/stand-with-workers?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123&city=&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=2+-+Sign+here+to+tell+Amazon+CEO+Jeff+Bezos+&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190715_2_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190715_2_1")
[STAND WITH AMAZON WORKERS](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/stand-with-workers?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123&city=&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=3+-+STAND+WITH+AMAZON+WORKERS&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190715_2_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190715_2_1")
Amazon's record with workers is shameful. subject: RE: Donald Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
Will you sign my petition to condemn Donald Trump's racism? I need 10,000 signatures today. >> | | |
---
| |
---
| | I'm simply appalled. Yesterday, President Trump told four Democratic Congresswomen of color to "go back" to where they came from. **Point blank: It was racist, and he is racist.** [_**I really need 10,000 Democrats on board -- condemn Donald Trump 's racism. >> **_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77db/2226625225/VEsH/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/condemn-trump-survey?source=20190715_em_pet_b2_castro_all") "Go back to Africa." "No Irish Need Apply." "Chinese Excluded." "Go back to Mexico." In every generation, Americans have faced those who made their political careers by dividing us. We are facing one again. We must stand together and condemn him. [_**Will you sign my petition to condemn Donald Trump 's racism? I need 10,000 signatures today. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77db/2226625225/VEsE/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/condemn-trump-survey?source=20190715_em_pet_b2_castro_all") I can't count the number of times I've been told, "Go back to Mexico." My grandma came from Mexico to America when she was 7, never made it past the 4th grade, and provided for us by cleaning houses. Two generations later, her grandson is running for President. That's what America stands for. And we refuse to let our own President get away with racist attacks on our citizens. [_**I need 10,000 signatures today. Condemn President Trump. Let 's stand strong in the face of racism. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77db/2226625225/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/condemn-trump-survey?source=20190715_em_pet_b2_castro_all") Thank you, Julian
---
| | | | | | | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77da/2226625225/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/JulianCastro")
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77d9/2226625225/VEsD/ "https://www.facebook.com/Julian-Castro-97458155742/")
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77d8/2226625225/VEsA/ "https://www.instagram.com/juliancastrotx/")
---
| | [](julianforthefuture.com)
---

---
| Contributions or gifts to Julian for the Future are not tax deductible.

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge:

Julian must be able to compete and build a movement that lasts for the long haul, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money to do it. Will you be a part of this historic campaign?

**[DONATE](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77c7/2226625225/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**
Again, we know we send you a lot of emails. We hope you'll stick with us long-term. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, please let us know [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77c6/2226625225/VEsO/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails"). If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce75/3eb3e2fc/589ad474/744a77c5/2226625225/VEsP/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/unsubscribe"), and we'll make sure to process your request.

This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com.

President Trump isn't just threatened by these congresswomen's diversity, he's threatened by their strength and determination to hold him and his administration accountable.
